title: Enhance Workform PDF Viewer: Omit Unanswered Questions & Embed Images
excerpt:
Hi Monday Community, 
We’ve been using Monday Forms extensively in our workflow. While the forms feature is incredibly useful, we’ve encountered a couple of limitations with the PDF viewer that we believe, if addressed, …

posts.author: Stan (Stan van Weringh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday Community,

We’ve been using Monday Forms extensively in our workflow. While the forms feature is incredibly useful, we’ve encountered a couple of limitations with the PDF viewer that we believe, if addressed, could significantly enhance the user experience for many teams.

Feature Requests:

  1. Removing unanswered questions: Currently, when exporting forms to PDF, any unanswered questions are displayed as “[This question is not answered]”. It would be great to have an option to completely omit these unanswered questions from the PDF. This would help in producing a cleaner and more streamlined document.
  2. Displaying images in PDF view: At present, images in the forms are exported as links in the PDF rather than being embedded directly. The images are also not downloadable for people outside Monday. Embedding images directly within the PDF would improve the readability and presentation of the exported forms.

These enhancements would greatly improve the functionality of the Workform PDF viewer, making it more efficient and user-friendly.

posts.author: Flowtech (Flowtech Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for sharing such a thoughtful and well-articulated suggestion — these are real pain points we hear often from teams using forms to generate documents.

The good news? There’s already a solution that tackles both of your feature requests — it’s called DocCreate, a native monday.com app that automates PDF generation using form or board data.

Here’s how DocCreate addresses your needs:

1. Skip Unanswered Questions

With DocCreate, you can conditionally hide empty answers so that only filled-in fields appear in your final PDF — no more [This question is not answered] clutter!

2. Embed Images Directly

DocCreate embeds images directly into your PDFs from file or image columns — perfect for inspection forms, client uploads, or product submissions. And yes — they’re visible and downloadable even outside of monday.com!

Bonus Features:

  • Automate PDF generation right after a form is submitted
  • Save documents into file columns
  • Customize layout with logos, fonts, colors, and page breaks
  • Send the PDF via email automatically
  • Supports connected boards, subitems, and mirrored data
